displayTitle: Make the Signal Usable, Then Make It Provable

beats.narration: You picked a frequency and set the drive. Now make the returning signal usable on screen — and then make it provable to an auditor.

payload.title: GAIN & STANDARDIZATION
payload.sub: First make the signal usable. Then make it provable.

beats.narration: Channel gain is receiver-side amplification, marked in decibels. Six dB doubles the displayed amplitude; twelve quadruples it. So twenty percent to eighty? Add twelve.
But gain lifts the flaw response and the noise by the exact same factor. The grass grows with the notch, so it never buys signal-to-noise.

payload.heading: Gain cannot buy signal-to-noise
payload.note: SAME NOTCH, 12 dB STEPS — NOISE SCALES IDENTICALLY

beats.narration: Standardization ties the screen to truth. Null on a matching reference standard, rotate lift-off horizontal, scan the notches, set gain to the required height.
Then verify on schedule and after any change. If a check fails, re-standardize and re-examine every part tested since the last valid check.

payload.sub: The interval between checks is the size of your re-work.
